data.title: India cracks down on crypto tax evaders as thousands receive notices - report
data.description:
India's top direct tax authority is cracking down on crypto tax evasion and money laundering, according to a media report published Friday.

The Central Board of Direct Taxes has flagged India-based people and businesses hiding crypto transactions that should be taxed at a flat 30% rate, an unidentified official said, Bloomberg reported.

Many individuals have been caught avoiding crypto taxes, prompting tax authorities to email thousands suspected of being in default, the official added.

data.title: Enphase Energy most shorted S&P 500 technology stock in May; Apple least shorted
data.description:
Short sellers increased their bets against the information technology sector in May. The sector which holds the highest weightage on the S&P had an average short interest of 3.17% in May, up from 1.78% at the end of April. 

This month, there were several new additions to the S&P 500 Technology sector as part of its quarterly rebalance. 

The S&P 500’s information technology sector (NYSEARCA:XLK) has grown 3.39% so far this year, compared to a 2.78% rise in the broader benchmark index. 

Enphase Energy (ENPH) was the most shorted stock in the sector in May, while Apple (AAPL) took the spot as the least shorted stock.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Enphase Energy (ENPH), Super Micro Computer (SMCI), and First Solar (FSLR) were the top three most shorted stocks in May. Short interest in ENPH rose to 19.27% in May, from 13.34% at the end of April. 

While, Super Micro Computer (SMCI), and First Solar (FSLR) had a short interest of 18.85%, and 10.58% respectively. 

Apple (AAPL) was the least shorted stock in the sector last month, with a short interest of 0.64%, preceded by Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL), with short interests of 0.79% and 0.96% respectively. 

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Technology Hardware, Storage, and Peripherals was the most shorted industry within the information technology sector, with 6.56% short interest in May, up from 2.18% in April.  

Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment was the second most shorted industry with 3.29% short interest as of May 31, compared to 1.69% at the end of April. 

Electronic Manufacturing Services was the least shorted industry in the sector this month, with a short interest of 1.19%.

data.title: Google sues LATAM Airlines in the U.S. over demands to take down YouTube videos
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is suing LATAM Airlines (NYSE:LTM) in the U.S. in response to a legal action from the airline after the company refused to take down YouTube videos that accused the airline's employee of sexual abuse against a child, according to a court filing from Thursday.

The lawsuit said LATAM, through its Brazilian subsidiary, sued Google in 2018 and demanded the tech giant take down two videos uploaded by U.S. citizen Raymond Moreira.

The company is requesting the court in San Jose, California, to declare that LATAM cannot force Google to remove the videos in the U.S.

A Brazilian appeals court will decide by next week whether it can order Google to take down the videos globally.

Moreira uploaded the videos in 2018 that allegedly show him interviewing his six-year-old son, who described abuse he experienced from a LATAM employee while traveling as an unaccompanied minor from Brazil to Florida.

data.title: Defense stocks flying high after Israel air strikes on Iran raise potential for wider war
data.description:
Defense stocks are rallying in Friday's trading after Israel launched a wave of military strikes against Iranian nuclear sites, missile factories and military leadership, raising fears of a wider Middle East conflict.

Israel Prime Minister Netanyahu said more attacks could be expected over the coming days as the country seeks to knock out Iran's nuclear program, and President Trump urged Iran to accept a U.S.-brokered nuclear deal to end the violence "before it is too late."

Lockeed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC), and RTX Corp. (RTX) - which all supply weapons to Israel through their contracts with the U.S. government - traded +3.6%, +3.5% and +3.2%, respectively, all ranking in the top 15 of Friday's S&P 500 leaderboard.

Among other relevant stocks, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) +2.8%, Kratos Defense (KTOS) +2.4%, AeroVironment (AVAV) +2.3%, L3Harris (LHX) +2.1%, General Dynamics (GD) +1.6%, Axon Enterprises (AXON) +1.2%, TransDigm (TDG) +1.2%, Heico (HEI) +0.8%, Howmet Aerospace (HWM) +0.5%, Textron (TXT) +0.5%.

Exceptions to the day's defense stock gains: Boeing (BA) -1.5% and GE Aerospace (GE) -1.1% in the wake of Thursday's Air India crash.

ETFs: (BATS:ITA), (PPA), (XAR), (DFEN)

data.title: U.S. stocks cut some losses as traders keep a close eye on Israel-Iran developments
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday pared back a chunk of their losses, though sentiment overall remained subdued after Israel attacked some of Iran's nuclear sites and military targets, raising fears of an all-out war in the Middle East.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) fell as much as -1.2%, then trimmed that decline and was last -0.4% in midday trade. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.5%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.9%.

Israel late on Thursday hit Iran with a series of what it called "preemptive" strikes. Iran retaliated by launching over 100 drones at Israel overnight. Israeli airspace has been closed, and a state of emergency has been declared amid expectations of another counterattack.

WTI crude oil futures (CL1:COM) and Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) - the international oil benchmark - surged in response, last +5.6% and +5.5%, respectively. Energy stocks rose in tandem, and the sector was easily the top gainer on Friday.  

“In economic and financial terms, Israel's attack on Iran constitutes a negative shock at an already fragile time. With growth and inflation concerns already high, key policy tools limited, and the global architecture shaken, the adverse effects are likely to be both direct and indirect,” said Mohamed El-Erian, part-time chief economic advisor at Allianz, on X (formerly Twitter).

“The severity of the impact will depend on the magnitude and duration of the attack, as well as the retaliation it triggers,” El-Erian said.

Over in the bond market, yields were higher. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 6 basis points to 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) also rose 6 basis points to 3.98%.

Friday's economic calendar was light. The consumer sentiment index rose to 60.5 in June, its first improvement in six months, from 52.2 in May, and exceeding the 53.5 consensus, according to data released by the University of Michigan.

“Usually, such a set of favorable UMich consumer sentiment numbers would attract quite a bit of attention,” El-Erian added. “The worry, however, is that the positive these numbers suggest may be overwhelmed by the surge in oil prices and worsening economic uncertainty resulting from the latest developments in the Middle East.”

Turning to active movers, Photoshop maker Adobe (ADBE) was -5% despite reporting a beat-and-raise quarter.

data.title: US energy independence has muted impact of oil volatility – Jeremy Siegel
data.description:
Recent Middle East tensions are having a more muted market effect than similar events would have caused in previous decades thanks to the U.S.’s energy independence, said Jeremy Siegel, Wharton professor and WisdomTree chief economist.

In an interview with CNBC, Siegel noted that despite Israel’s military actions and the potential for escalation, financial markets have shown relative resilience with equities remaining only about 3% below all-time highs.

“Had this happened five years ago, ten, twenty years ago, you would see a much greater reaction in the market,” Siegel said. He attributed this limited market response to Iran’s weakened position both internally and through its diminished proxy forces, suggesting the timing of recent military actions was strategically sound.

Oil prices have responded more dramatically, with WTI crude (CL1:COM) rising 5.6% and Brent crude (CO1:COM) up 5.5%, though Siegel downplayed these increases. He pointed out that even with the spike, prices remain comparable to levels from just a few months ago following recent declines of $10-12 per barrel, and only a more extreme scenario would significantly impact markets: “Obviously, closing the Gulf [would be] something that would really be radical… some people had talked about maybe $100 and that would be really quite devastating.”

The economist emphasized that U.S. energy independence has fundamentally changed how oil price fluctuations affect the American economy.

“The fact that the United States now has energy independence, which it did not in the past, really mutes the effect on the United States because although the users are going to be charged more, the producers are going to get more,” Siegel said.

Regarding market valuations, Siegel defended current levels as reasonable despite geopolitical and trade uncertainties.

“Once you exclude the Magnificent Seven (MAGS), you’re 20 or slightly under. I’ve always argued that that’s pretty much a fair valuation,” he stated, while noting markets are assuming tariffs won’t significantly worsen.

Siegel suggested companies are finding ways “to increase efficiency, maybe with AI and other measures to offset some of the higher costs so that profit margins can still go up in the future.”

Investors can track more oil ETFs, here: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), (USL)

data.title: Bets against utility stocks marginally decrease in May; AES most shorted
data.description:
Bets against Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU) marginally decreased in May compared to the end of April, with AES Corp (AES) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Sempra (SRE) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 utilities stocks was 2.35% of shares float at the end of May, slightly down from 2.36% at the end of April.

The S&P 500’s Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU) was up 8.19% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P500 market, which gained 2.78% during the same period.

Stocks with the least and largest short positions

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

AES Corp (AES) had 43.70 million shares sold short as of May 31, or 6.17% of shares float.

Evergy (EVRG) was the second-most shorted stock with short interest of 4.72% followed by CenterPoint Energy (CNP) with 4.60%, American Electric Power (AEP) with 3.92% and WEC Energy Group (WEC) with 3.58% and.

Sempra (SRE) was the least shorted stock, with 6.58 million shares sold short or 1.01% of the shares float, followed by PG&E (PCG) and Duke Energy (DUK) with short interest of 1.21% and 1.25% respectively.

Industry analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Independent Power & Renewable Electricity Producers was the most shorted industry within the sector with 4.84% short interest as of May end, compared to 4.50% a month ago.

Gas Utilities stood at the second spot with 3.50% short interest as of the end of May, which increased from 3.33% at the end of April.

Multi utilities stocks took the third spot with 2.66% short interest as of May end, compared to 2.49% at the end of March.

Bets against the Electric Utilities sector as a whole have decreased to 1.98% at the end of May, compared to 2.11% a month ago.

data.title: Carvana shares 'due for a pause,' Q2 EBITDA could miss expectations - J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Carvana’s (NYSE:CVNA) meteoric 187% appreciation year-over-year is “due for a pause” after materially outperforming peers, with the car retailer facing larger-than-expected compressed gain-on-sale (“GoS”) margins based on recent prime deal disclosures, said J. P. Morgan analysts Rajat Gupta.

Fitch recently noted that CRVNA 2025-P2 auto receivables trust – which is backed by pools of prime auto loans – has a weighted average FICO score of 701, which is on the lower end relative to peer prime issuers. In addition, the offering’s percentage of extended-term loans of 61 or more months is elevated at 95.3% and loans with terms of more than 72 months comprised 47.9% of the pool, higher than most comparable transactions.

“It’s important to note that Carvana is not immune to recession risks but more importantly, [gain on sale] margins booked today already take into account ongoing loss performance across Carvana and industry with elevated return expectations baked in for residual tranche buyers,” Gupta said in Friday’s note to investors.  

Gupta also warns that the second quarter could be the first time in five quarters that the company’s EBITDA will miss Wall Street’s expectations. For the first quarter, Carvana (CNVA) reported record retail units sold, record profitability, and record net income margin.

Although Gupta maintained his Overweight rating on the stock, his cautious tone and prediction of a Q2 miss is weighing on Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) shares, last traded with a loss of 6% and its second day in negative territory.

data.title: Visa, Mastercard stocks slide after stablecoin report
data.description:
Mastercard (NYSE:MA) stock dropped 4.9% and Visa (NYSE:V) sank 5.3% in Friday late morning trading after a report said massive retailers Amazon.com (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT) are considering stablecoin payment systems that would bypass traditional payment rails.

Some fintechs also weakened, with PayPal Holdings (PYPL) wilting 4.4%, Paysafe (PSFE) dropping 3.8%, Shift4 Payments (FOUR) declining 3.7%, and Block (XYZ) dipping 2.1%.

The Israeli attack on Iran is likely contributing to investors' concerns about the economy, in general, and financial stocks, in particular. The S&P 500 Financials Sector Index was the largest decliner in Friday trading, down 1.5%. The Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX) fell 1.2%.

"A stagflationary growth lower/inflation higher shock from higher oil on top of Trump tariffs would further deteriorate the outlook and complicate the task of central bankers," Evercore ISI's Krishna Guha wrote in a note to clients.

Credit card names American Express (AXP) lost 2.1%, Bread Financial (BFH) -2.8%, and Synchrony Financial (SYF) -1.3%.
